pattern: Alternating the two same-letter blocks caps how many of each you can safely use together, and the mixed block is always free to add, which reduces the whole problem to a small counting argument.
hints: "AA" and "BB" are risky only when placed next to a block ending or starting with the same letter; "AB" never creates a run longer than one of either letter by itself.

You can always alternate "AA" and "BB" blocks together up to the smaller of the two available counts, use every "AB" block freely in between, and - only when one letter's supply is strictly larger - tack on exactly one extra block of the surplus letter at the very end.
Let m be the smaller of the two same-letter counts; the answer is 2 * (2*m + z) plus 2 more whenever the two same-letter counts differ, where z is the count of mixed blocks.

edge_inputs.description: the smallest possible counts of every block type
every count at its maximum and equal, no surplus bonus applies
one same-letter count at minimum, the other and the mixed count at maximum
equal large same-letter supplies but only one mixed block, tempts an off-by-one in the alternation count
heavily unequal same-letter supplies with a moderate mixed count
small equal counts of every block type

stress.description: random x, y, z drawn independently across the full allowed range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    x = rng.randint(1, 50)
    y = rng.randint(1, 50)
    z = rng.randint(1, 50)
    return [x, y, z]
